# clipandnote

> A fast, native macOS screenshot-markup app — a modern, open take on Skitch. Capture from the menu bar, annotate with movable objects, and export real layered files. Free for personal use, 100% on-device, no account.

clipandnote lives in your menu bar. Grab a region, window, or the full screen, then mark it up in a lightweight editor where every annotation stays a live, editable object (not flattened into the image). It is signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple.

## Key features
- Capture from the menu bar: crosshair region, previous area, window, fullscreen, or a menu — with global hotkeys (default ⌥⌘ combos).
- Annotate with movable objects: arrows, lines, boxes, ellipses, freehand pen, text, highlighter — each one selectable, restylable, and re-orderable.
- Ruler and angle tools for exact pixel distances and degrees.
- Pixelate / blur to hide sensitive details (tokens, emails, faces).
- Grab Text (OCR) and automatic snapshot naming run 100% on-device — no cloud, no account.
- Multi-page PDFs: open a PDF, mark up every page in one window (with a thumbnail sidebar), and export it all as one layered PDF.
- Export editable documents (.can), or layered PDF, PNG, and SVG; copy a flattened result straight to the clipboard.
- Automatic local history of recent markups, one click away in the menu bar.

## Details
- Platform: macOS 13 (Ventura) or later, Apple Silicon and Intel.
- Price: free for personal use.
- License: CC BY-NC 4.0 (open, non-commercial).
- Privacy: on-device only; no telemetry beyond privacy-friendly, cookieless page analytics.

- Source / GitHub: https://github.com/mrmichaelmoorecom-sys/clipandnote

- Sibling app (clipboard manager): https://clipandcue.com
